item.name: Global System for Mobile Communications Creates a new TrafficInfluence resource

request.name: Global System for Mobile Communications Creates a new TrafficInfluence resource

description.content: Takes as input an object containing the intents from the API Consumer and creates a TrafficInfluence resourse accordingly. The trafficInfluenceID parameter, that is part of the object, must not be valorized when creating a new resource. For this reason the trafficInfluenceID parameter must be avoided in the object, anyway it will be ignored by the API Producer. It is automatically generated by the system and returned in the response.

variable.description: API root for the Traffic Influence API

header.description: Correlation id for the different services.

response.name: TrafficInfluence resource created, the related object is returned with the resource ID (trafficInfluenceID) and status (state) valorised.

request.name: Global System for Mobile Communications updates a specific TrafficInfluence resource, identified by the trafficInfluenceID value.

description.content: The resource identified by the trafficInfluenceID value can be modified. Before the PATCH can be invoked the status (state) of the resource must be "active". If not an errore code 409 (DENIED_WAIT) is returned.

API root for the Traffic Influence API

description.content: API root for the Traffic Influence API

info.name: Global System for Mobile Communications GSMA Camara Project Traffic Influence API

description.content:
The Traffic Influence API provides the capability to establish the optimal routing, in terms of latency, in a specific geographical area, between the user device, e.g. the users smartphone, and the optimal Edge Application Server (EAS) instance nearby. If the user device is detected by the developer to have moved into a different geographical location, the Traffic Influence API can be invoked again to get the optimal routing in the new location.

Contact Support:
 Email: project-email@sample.com
